# UC-19.1.14 · UCS Manager Backup Validation (All Configuration, Full State, and Target-File Integrity)

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** IT Operations &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Configuration &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We check that the master copy of your data hall switch-and-server settings is being saved on time, and that each saved file is big enough to be a real full copy, not a blank stub. If a save is late, missing, or the wrong size compared to what you usually get, we let the people who run that hall know before a bad day becomes a long rebuild of everything by hand.*

---

## Description

Validates that Cisco UCS Manager’s scheduled exports of **All configuration**, **Full state**, and related policy types actually ran inside each policy’s own interval, and that the on-disk file at the **remote** path is neither suspiciously small nor wildly larger than a stable 30-day median. The mechanism pairs **`mgmtBackup` JSON** (real `lastBackup` timestamps and `type`) from a `/nuova` HEC poller you operate with a **second stream** that **stats the remote file** and joins on **remoteFile**, then applies `ucs_backup_baseline.csv` thresholds. This is distinct from a generic “syslog only” use case: you can prove *when* the management plane thought a backup last succeeded, *what* it intended to copy (full state versus logical), and *whether* a file of plausible size really appeared at the off-box target.

## Value

UCSM is the single system of record for service profiles, pools, vNIC and vHBA policy, boot order, and org-wide names that an entire B-series, X-series, or C-series service-profile estate depends on. Losing the management database without a recent **All configuration** (and often **Full state** for a true one-click rebuild) means days of hand reconstruction even when the blades are healthy. Compliance teams, too, need evidence that a scheduled export that meets their control frequency actually **completed** and **landed** off the management cluster—not merely that a policy is enabled. By correlating **UCSM’s own `lastBackup` clock** to **file size on the SFTP or NFS side**, you catch the worst silent failures: wrong credentials, path typos, zero-byte transfers that still tick a timestamp, or a profile explosion that should have doubled export size. This use case is the ITGC-friendly bridge that turns “we back up the fabric managers” from a line item in a control sheet into a **reproducible** Splunk evidence pack your storage and app teams can trust during a P1 on a different day.

## Implementation

Package the HEC poller in a tiny TA on a Heavy Forwarder, land JSON as `cisco:ucs:mgmtbackup` and `cisco:ucs:backuppolicy`, deploy the filesystem forwarder on the export target, load `ucs_backup_baseline.csv` and `ucs_backup_policy_owners.csv` in your governance app, then save the `spl` as a 30-minute alert over `-2d@d`, throttle 24h per `policy_name`, and route CRITICAL|WARNING to the platform pager with ServiceNow ticket payload.

## Evidence

Primary evidence is the saved search `uc_19_1_14_ucsm_backup_governance` (or your org naming) plus a weekly CSV to a restricted, ITGC-controlled share that records `ucsm_domain`, `policy_name`, `severity_bucket`, and `file_size_mb` for auditors. The underlying events are the JSON lines indexed as `cisco:ucs:mgmtbackup` and `cisco:ucs:backuppolicy` (HEC from the poller) plus the companion `cisco:ucs:backup:filesystem` JSON lines (or a forwarder on `linux:syslog` with the same fields if you must keep sourcetype stock). Retain 13 months in `index=cisco_ucs` to align with common ITGC back-up control sampling windows, or your legal minimum, whichever is longer.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- One **Heavy Forwarder** (or any host licensed for a Python scripted input) that can reach the UCSM **virtual IP** on **443/tcp** and your **HEC** endpoint on the indexer/HEC host **8088** (or the Cloud HEC global URL). HEC token stored in a password manager and in **splunk.secret**-protected stanzas, **not** in Git.
- A read-only service account in UCSM with rights to `org-root` and the **“Operations”** or equivalent role that your security team will allow to read `mgmtBackup` and backup **policy** objects. Separate credentials for the target SFTP/SCP account if the security team will not let the poller also write test files; read-only to `lastBackup` is enough; writes go through UCSM, not the poller.
- **index=cisco_ucs** (or your org standard) with a role for the UCS platform SREs that is **not** `admin`.
- A working **UCSM syslog** path (SC4S or a syslog server with a forwarder) so you can backstop numeric polling with `cisco:ucs:syslog` when Cisco emits `%UCSM-6-AUDIT` for policy DN changes. This is **not** the primary numeric field source for `lastBackup`—it is a forensics and triage **second opinion** when you must explain an alert to a human being.

### Step 1 — Identify (why this matters, what “good” looks like, and which backup types to treat as first-class)
Cisco UCS Manager is the **only** place where **service profile** definitions, **vNIC/vHBA** placement, **boot policy** wiring, and **pools of identities** live as one coherent, exportable state. A healthy blade can still be **unbuildable in a new fabric** if you lose the UCSM database. From a TAC and partner perspective, a recent **All configuration** export is the first artifact you hand over when a fabric is corrupted, and a **Full state** export is the **largest, most complete** picture for a **catastrophic** management-plane loss—at the cost of a big binary. **System configuration** is usually **under one megabyte** and covers admin users, NTP, and communications services, while **Logical configuration** (VLAN, VSAN, addresses, and identifiers) is often a few megabytes to a few **tens** of megabytes on wide estates. **Size matters** not because a single number tells you the fabric is healthy, but because **a sudden drop** to a few kilobytes after months of **multi‑megabyte** All-configuration exports is almost always a **path or permission** failure, and an unexplained **doubling** after **no** template or pool work often means a **duplicate export**, **stale `remoteFile`**, or a **new org import** the baselines have not yet absorbed. A plain **UCSM GUI** “success” is not enough to satisfy a SOX, HIPAA, or internal ITGC control that asks: “prove the off-site file exists, on schedule, in the right size window.” The Splunk side must therefore combine **(a)** a UCSM-native clock (`lastBackup` from `mgmtBackup` via XML) with **(b)** a host-side or filer-side **stat** of the `remoteFile` the MO names. Without (b) you are blind to a successful-looking timestamp that did not actually result in a **non‑trivial** file at rest; without (a) you are blind to policy intent—disabled schedules, one-time jobs, and type changes.
The **CIM** mapping is deliberately paired to **Change** and **Inventory**: each successful export and each schedule shift is a **change** to the **inventory** of what you would restore in a DR exercise. The alert’s job is to catch **unplanned drift** in that change stream and in the on-disk **inventory of backup artifacts**.
Day-two operators should expect three colour bands. **All configuration** is the one most application owners implicitly assume “exists” when they say “our UCS is in backup.” **Full state** is heavier and is often **weekly**; missing one window is a warning if your policy is daily—tune `expected_interval_hours` in the lookup to match the **Cisco** schedule, not a spreadsheet from five years ago. **System** and **Logical** are still compliance-relevant, but a miss there is often a side effect of a disabled policy during a change window, not a fleet-wide network outage—route those through the **suppression** path instead of a broad mute.
Finally, the reason `inventory` in CIM is appropriate is that a backup object is, in effect, a **documented** asset you would hand to a regulator, insurer, or internal auditor; Splunk is your **WORM-adjacent** record that the file has been in the right band this quarter.

### Step 2 — Implement (HEC poller, filesystem side, transforms, and lookups you can paste)
**(A) Python scripted input (shape only—pin `requests` and `urllib3` in your venv, not system Python 2).** The script is installed under `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA_ucsm_backup_hec/bin/ucsm_mgmtbackup_poll.py` and is invoked on an interval. It performs **aaaLogin** to obtain a **cookie** `ucsm-cookie=…`, then posts XML:
`configResolveClass cookie="$COOKIE" inHierarchical=”false” classId="mgmtBackup"` and optionally `inHierarchical=”true”` to walk children if your MO tree requires it, then optionally `classId=”firmwareBackup”` or `firmwareESBackup` only if you want separate sourcetypes. Parse each `mgmtBackup` with `xml.etree.ElementTree`, and for every MO emit one JSON line with at least: `sourcetype` fixed string `cisco:ucs:mgmtbackup`, `ucsm_fqdn`, `name`, `type`, `lastBackup`, `nextBackup`, `remoteFile`, `hostname`, `proto`, `userId` (or a redacted `userId` if you must), `adminState`, `lifecycle`, `dn`, and `ingest_tier="prod"` if you have tiers. The script must **not** log secrets. Use a **15-minute** `interval` to line up with reasonable polling cost; UCSM is not a per-second time series.
`aaaRefresh` the cookie every ~25 minutes in the same long-lived process, and `aaaLogout` on shutdown. On HTTP 401/403, emit a `severity="ingest_degraded"` event with **no** MO rows so the absence-of-data alert (see Step 3) can fire.
**(B) `inputs.conf` on the Heavy Forwarder app `TA_ucsm_backup_hec/local/inputs.conf`:**

```ini
[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA_ucsm_backup_hec/bin/ucsm_mgmtbackup_poll.py]
interval = 900
sourcetype = cisco:ucs:mgmtbackup
source = UCSM:mgmtbackup:HEC
disabled = 0
index = cisco_ucs
passAuth = false

[script://$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA_ucsm_backup_hec/bin/ucsm_backup_policy_poll.py]
interval = 3600
sourcetype = cisco:ucs:backuppolicy
source = UCSM:backuppolicy:HEC
disabled = 0
index = cisco_ucs
```

(Second script can be a lighter class.) Restart `splunkd` after the first deploy and tail `splunkd.log` for `ExecProcessor` with `rc=0`.
**(C) `props.conf` for the JSON you emit to file before `batch` send, or for HEC-raw if you set `sourcetype` at insert time (preferred: set in HEC `fields`):**

```ini
# props.conf (local) — HEC: prefer passing epoch time in the JSON; if parsing _raw, keep TIME_* minimal.
[cisco:ucs:mgmtbackup]
KV_MODE = json
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
TRUNCATE = 1048576
MAX_TIMESTAMP_LOOKAHEAD = 32
LINE_BREAKER = ([\r\n]+)
CHARSET = UTF-8
TIME_FORMAT = %Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N
TZ = UTC
# Optional: if _time must come from lastBackup in the body, set TIME_PREFIX to a regex that
# immediately precedes the timestamp string (sample pattern; validate against your _raw).
# TIME_PREFIX = lastBackup[\\s]*:[\\s]*"
```

If your JSON `lastBackup` is null and you fall back to `_time=now` at ingest, the alert in Step 2 will be wrong: never inject a false `now()`—emit **no** `lastBackup` field when UCSM has never run yet and let the join show `null` (that is a **CRITICAL** by age rule once `expected_interval_hours` elapses). Add `EVAL-last_epoch = if(isnotnull('lastBackup'),strptime(…` only if you must rescue legacy samples—match the public `spl` instead.
**(D) Filesystem poller (SFTP/SCP target).** A tiny Python script runs from **cron** every **10** minutes, walks the explicit file paths you keep in a **local** YAML list that mirrors `remoteFile` values you care about, `os.path.getsize` each file, and appends a JSON line to `/var/log/ucs_backup_check.log` on the file server. The Universal Forwarder monitors that file with:

```ini
[monitor:///var/log/ucs_backup_check.log]
disabled = 0
sourcetype = cisco:ucs:backup:filesystem
index = cisco_ucs
```

`props` uses `KV_MODE = json` and the same `TZ` as the writer host (prefer UTC in the log line: `"observed_time":"2026-04-25T10:00:00Z"`).
**(E) `transforms.conf` (in the same governance app) for the CSVs:**

```
[ucs_backup_baseline]
filename = ucs_backup_baseline.csv

case_sensitive_match = false

[ucs_backup_policy_owners]
filename = ucs_backup_policy_owners.csv
case_sensitive_match = false
```

`collections.conf` is **not** required for file lookups.
**(F) Lookup `lookups/ucs_backup_baseline.csv` (header **must** be present, UTF-8, no BOM):**
`ucsm_domain,policy_name,expected_interval_hours,baseline_median_mb,baseline_p90_mb,notes
`
`ucs-prod-01.example.com,backup-policy-default,24,18.2,24.1,all-configuration to SFTP; rotate median monthly
ucs-prod-01.example.com,dr-fullstate-weekly,168,120.0,180.0,full-state; warn on huge delta after org import
ucslab-03.example.com,test-logical,168,2.0,2.4,low churn lab org; do not page prod on-call with this row
`

**(G) `lookups/ucs_backup_policy_owners.csv` sample:**
`ucsm_domain,policy_name,business_service,oncall_pager,team_email,runbook_url
`
`ucs-prod-01.example.com,backup-policy-default,Prod UCS platforms,+15555550100,ucs-pager@example.com,https://runbooks.example.local/ucsm/backup
ucs-prod-01.example.com,dr-fullstate-weekly,Prod DR evidence,,dr-evidence@example.com,https://runbooks.example.local/ucsm/dr
`
`runbook_url` may be a placeholder the parent team replaces.

**Primary SPL to save as a report/alert (must match the `spl` field verbatim, including newlines, when pasted into a ```spl block):**

```spl
// UC-19.1.14 — UCS Manager Backup Validation. Joins UCSM mgmtBackup MO (cisco:ucs:mgmtbackup) policy/last-run truth with off-box file integrity (cisco:ucs:backup:filesystem). Severity tiers: CRITICAL when miss_ratio>2 OR file_size_bytes<100KB OR pct_of_baseline<50; WARNING for one missed interval or size drift; otherwise INFO.
index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:mgmtbackup" earliest=-2d@d
| eval ucsm_domain=coalesce(ucsm_fqdn,ucsmHost,host)
| eval policy_name=coalesce(name,n,"")
| eval policy_type=coalesce(type,backup_type,"unspecified")
| eval last_str=coalesce(lastBackup,last_backup,"")
| eval t1=if(len(last_str)>0,strptime(last_str,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N"),null())
| eval t2=if(isnull(t1) AND len(last_str)>0,strptime(last_str,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"),null())
| eval last_epoch=coalesce(lastBackupEpoch,last_backup_epoch,t1,t2)
| eval backup_age_seconds=if(isnotnull(last_epoch),now()-last_epoch,null())
| eval last_backup_age_hours=round(backup_age_seconds/3600,3)
| eval remote_key=coalesce(remoteFile,remote_file,"")
| lookup ucs_backup_baseline.csv ucsm_domain policy_name OUTPUT expected_interval_hours AS exp_h, baseline_median_mb, baseline_p90_mb
| eval exp_h=if(isnotnull(exp_h) AND exp_h>0,exp_h,24)
| join type=left max=0 remote_key [ search index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:backup:filesystem" earliest=-2d@d | eval remote_key=coalesce(remoteFile,remote_file,target_path) | stats latest(_time) AS fs_time latest(file_size_bytes) AS file_size_bytes by remote_key ]
| fillnull value=0 file_size_bytes
| eval file_size_mb=round(file_size_bytes/1024/1024,4)
| eval miss_ratio=if(isnotnull(backup_age_seconds) AND isnotnull(exp_h),(backup_age_seconds/3600)/exp_h,null())
| eval pct_of_baseline=if(isnotnull(baseline_median_mb) AND baseline_median_mb>0,round(100*file_size_mb/baseline_median_mb,1),null())
| eval severity_bucket=case((isnotnull(miss_ratio) AND miss_ratio>2) OR (file_size_bytes<100000) OR (isnotnull(pct_of_baseline) AND pct_of_baseline<50),"CRITICAL",(isnotnull(miss_ratio) AND miss_ratio>1 AND miss_ratio<=2) OR (isnotnull(pct_of_baseline) AND pct_of_baseline>150),"WARNING",1=1,"INFO")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity_bucket=="CRITICAL" AND file_size_bytes<100000,"Verify target path permissions, VRF/DNS, and that the scheduled export finished; re-run on-demand All Configuration to a test filename.",severity_bucket=="CRITICAL" AND (miss_ratio>2),"Check UCSM backup policy, scheduler, and FSM; read matching events in cisco:ucs:syslog for FSM or audit failure.",severity_bucket=="WARNING" AND (pct_of_baseline>150),"Review recent template or org object adds; recompute baselines if scope grew legitimately.",severity_bucket=="WARNING","Investigate one missed interval, poll skew, or slow copy; compare to change window.",1=1,"Continue baseline collection; no alert.")
| lookup ucs_backup_policy_owners.csv ucsm_domain policy_name OUTPUT business_service,oncall_pager,team_email,runbook_url
| table ucsm_domain,policy_name,policy_type,last_backup_age_hours,exp_h,file_size_mb,baseline_median_mb,pct_of_baseline,severity_bucket,recommended_action,miss_ratio,remote_key,business_service,oncall_pager,team_email,runbook_url
| sort 0 severity_bucket, - last_backup_age_hours
```

Alert **clone** of the same SPL, before `table`, add `| where severity_bucket IN ("CRITICAL","WARNING")` for the actual triggered saved search. Keep the full unfiltered run as a compliance dashboard for auditors.

### Step 3 — Validate (SPL, lookups, and live fault injection)
- **Sourcetype reality check:**
` | metadata type=sourcetypes index=cisco_ucs` — confirm `cisco:ucs:mgmtbackup` and `cico:ucs:backup:filesystem` (typo) should **not** appear: you want `cisco:ucs:backup:filesystem`. If you do not see the latter, the forwarder is not yet tailing the JSON log, or the index is misspelled. Fix that before a single alert.
`index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:mgmtbackup" earliest=-1d | head 1 | fieldsummary` — verify `lastBackup` exists and the parser kept **UTC**; if you see a **blank** `last_str` after deploy, the MO did not return `lastBackup` and you are in a **true first-run** state—treat the **CRITICAL** path only after the expected interval, not the first 15 minutes of life.
- **Lookup presence:**
` | inputlookup ucs_backup_baseline.csv` and `| inputlookup ucs_backup_policy_owners.csv` in the `Search` app should return the CSV rows. If the knowledge manager sees `Lookup table does not exist`, the CSV is in the **User** app, not the **system** or **governance** app: move it and **Settings → Lookups** → set **permission** to the roles that need it.
- **Ad-hoc dry run of the main SPL** with `earliest=-1d latest=now` in **Verbose** and confirm: `join` is not **empty** for at least one `remote_key` that has both MO row and `file_size_bytes>0`.
- **On-demand success path:** in UCSM, run an **on-demand** All configuration backup to a **test** `remoteFile` in the same `mgmtBackup` policy, wait **≤2×** your poll `interval` (e.g. 15–30 min), and confirm: `last_str` changes, `file_size_bytes` is **> 100000** in Splunk, and `severity_bucket` is **INFO** for that row once baselines are loaded.
- **Controlled failure path (lab only):** repoint a **non-production** `hostname` in the **SFTP** target to a known-bad **DNS** name and trigger the schedule; you should read `%UCSM-3-FSM_FAILED` or an `FSM:FAIL` in `cisco:ucs:syslog` if your syslog path is healthy, and your MO row will either show no `lastBackup` advance, or a **lastBackup** with `file_size_bytes<100000` at the file layer—`severity_bucket` should land **CRITICAL** after your interval and lookup thresholds, proving the end-to-end control.
**CLI checks before you trust the alert:**
` splunk btool props list cisco:ucs:mgmtbackup --debug | head` on the **indexer** or **HEC-heavy** to confirm the **TIME_FORMAT** won against other `props`.
` splunk search '| makeresults | eval x=strptime("2026-04-25T10:00:00.000Z","%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N") | eval age=now()-x'` to prove time math in your TZ.
On the file server, `ls -l /var/log/ucs_backup_check.log` and `tail -1` to eyeball JSON lines. On UCSM, `show backup status` in the **NX-OS** context is **not** the contract—UCSM is **not** a Nexus—always trust **GUI/API** in current 4.x doc names.

### Step 4 — Operationalize (saved searches, throttles, routing, and audit placement)
- Save **`uc_19_1_14_ucsm_backup_governance_dash`**: full `spl` as a **24h**-window panel for the compliance team, and a second saved search **`uc_19_1_14_alert`** with an appended `| where severity_bucket IN ("CRITICAL","WARNING")` on **30-minute** `cron` over **-2d@d** so long weekends still age correctly.
- **Throttle** use Splunk’s alert **throttle** UI on **`policy_name`** (and add **`ucsm_domain` in the `fields` list**) with **24h** to avoid duplicate **ServiceNow** **INC** rows when the file server is 10 minutes late but still copying.
- **Alert action:** two channels—**email** the **`team_email`** from the lookup, and a **ServiceNow** **mapped** **alert** that writes **short description** = `UCSM backup {policy_name} {severity_bucket}` and **assignment group** = platform storage/UCS, **not** the generic NOC, unless you also fan out a **P1** child when `policy_type` matches **"full-state"** in a financial-services RACI (tune in your `recommended_action` clone).
- **Runbook URL:** keep **`https://runbooks.example.com/ucsm/backup` as a placeholder` in the CSV until the parent team pastes a real Confluence/SharePoint page; do not page without a link the on-call can open from mobile. **(Replace `example` domains before production).**
- **Dashboard drilldown:** tokenize `ucsm_domain` into a form that opens a second dashboard with: (1) `cisco:ucs:syslog` search for `org-root/backup-policy` DNs, (2) a **UCSM FSM** deep-link to the right **dn** in the **Java/HTML** UI, (3) a table of the last 7 `file_size_mb` values from the filesystem stream.
- **ITSI (optional):** if you have **Splunk ITSI**, add a service metric **“UCSM configuration backup health”** that reads the **count of CRITICAL** from a **summary index** or **KPI** search; wire it to the business service in CMDB, not only to a generic **Linux** service.
- **Export cadence for auditors:** Monday **09:00** **CSV** of the dashboard table to a **WORM** or **S3 object lock** path with object tags **control_id=ITGC-7.3-Backup** or your org standard.

### Step 5 — Tune (baselines, suppression, and ethics of paging)
- **Every four production weeks,** run a scheduled report that recalculates `baseline_median_mb` and `baseline_p90_mb` per `policy_name` with `| stats median(file_size_mb) as baseline_median_mb perc90(file_size_mb) as baseline_p90_mb by ucsm_domain policy_name` on **INFO-only** **successful** **weeks** and **| outputlookup** to a **staging** CSV for human approval before you replace production baselines. Keep the prior month’s CSV in Git with a **tag** for audit diffs (no secrets in Git).
- **Daylight-savings and UTC:** re-run a one-time `| eval drift=_indextime-_time` on both streams; if the filesystem writer uses **local** time in JSON without a `Z` offset, you will get **one-hour** false `miss_ratio` blips. Fix the **writer** to **ISO-8601** **UTC** with a trailing **Z**.
- **Planned `adminState` off:** in change windows, add a row in **`ucs_suppressed_backup_policies.csv`** (optional second lookup) for `(ucsm_domain,policy_name,start_epoch,end_epoch,change_id)` and add `| join …` to null `severity_bucket` in that window after your governance review.
- **Over-tight `pct_of_baseline>150`:** a big **increase** is often a **blessed** new **service-profile** import—verify with the same **Change** **ticket** that introduced **200** new **profiles**; if yes, re-baseline, not a sev-1. **Under** `50%` is almost always a **true** path or **failed** **SFTP** **partial** copy—treat with suspicion until a human `ls` on the file server says otherwise.
- **Cultural tuning:** if your leadership refuses pages on `WARNING` for **Full state** in non-trading hours, add `| where NOT (policy_type="full-state" AND date_hour<7)` in a child alert only for that **business**—do not fork the public JSON in the library; do that in a **local** **overlay** so upgrades do not clobber you.
- **KPI health:** if `join` to filesystem is always **null** for **lab** (no file forwarder) but `mgmtBackup` is healthy, the row’s **CRITICAL** on **100KB** will false-positive. Either deploy the file-side forwarder, or set **`baseline_median_mb=0.001`** in **lab** with a **separate** **alert** that only checks `miss_ratio`—never ship that to prod JSON.

Closing: this UC is **not** a second copy of **UC-19.1.11** (service profile association), not **19.1.5** (LAN **port-channels**), and not **19.1.36** (vNIC A/B). It is the **governance and DR posture** of the **UCSM** **configuration** you would hand to a human being after a **real** **DISASTER**, not a **single** **bad** **boot** in **one** **host**. Keep it next to, not instead of, your **KVM** and **KVM** **password** break-glass procedures.


## SPL

```spl
// UC-19.1.14 — UCS Manager Backup Validation. Joins UCSM mgmtBackup MO (cisco:ucs:mgmtbackup) policy/last-run truth with off-box file integrity (cisco:ucs:backup:filesystem). Severity tiers: CRITICAL when miss_ratio>2 OR file_size_bytes<100KB OR pct_of_baseline<50; WARNING for one missed interval or size drift; otherwise INFO.
index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:mgmtbackup" earliest=-2d@d
| eval ucsm_domain=coalesce(ucsm_fqdn,ucsmHost,host)
| eval policy_name=coalesce(name,n,"")
| eval policy_type=coalesce(type,backup_type,"unspecified")
| eval last_str=coalesce(lastBackup,last_backup,"")
| eval t1=if(len(last_str)>0,strptime(last_str,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3N"),null())
| eval t2=if(isnull(t1) AND len(last_str)>0,strptime(last_str,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"),null())
| eval last_epoch=coalesce(lastBackupEpoch,last_backup_epoch,t1,t2)
| eval backup_age_seconds=if(isnotnull(last_epoch),now()-last_epoch,null())
| eval last_backup_age_hours=round(backup_age_seconds/3600,3)
| eval remote_key=coalesce(remoteFile,remote_file,"")
| lookup ucs_backup_baseline.csv ucsm_domain policy_name OUTPUT expected_interval_hours AS exp_h, baseline_median_mb, baseline_p90_mb
| eval exp_h=if(isnotnull(exp_h) AND exp_h>0,exp_h,24)
| join type=left max=0 remote_key [ search index=cisco_ucs sourcetype="cisco:ucs:backup:filesystem" earliest=-2d@d | eval remote_key=coalesce(remoteFile,remote_file,target_path) | stats latest(_time) AS fs_time latest(file_size_bytes) AS file_size_bytes by remote_key ]
| fillnull value=0 file_size_bytes
| eval file_size_mb=round(file_size_bytes/1024/1024,4)
| eval miss_ratio=if(isnotnull(backup_age_seconds) AND isnotnull(exp_h),(backup_age_seconds/3600)/exp_h,null())
| eval pct_of_baseline=if(isnotnull(baseline_median_mb) AND baseline_median_mb>0,round(100*file_size_mb/baseline_median_mb,1),null())
| eval severity_bucket=case((isnotnull(miss_ratio) AND miss_ratio>2) OR (file_size_bytes<100000) OR (isnotnull(pct_of_baseline) AND pct_of_baseline<50),"CRITICAL",(isnotnull(miss_ratio) AND miss_ratio>1 AND miss_ratio<=2) OR (isnotnull(pct_of_baseline) AND pct_of_baseline>150),"WARNING",1=1,"INFO")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity_bucket=="CRITICAL" AND file_size_bytes<100000,"Verify target path permissions, VRF/DNS, and that the scheduled export finished; re-run on-demand All Configuration to a test filename.",severity_bucket=="CRITICAL" AND (miss_ratio>2),"Check UCSM backup policy, scheduler, and FSM; read matching events in cisco:ucs:syslog for FSM or audit failure.",severity_bucket=="WARNING" AND (pct_of_baseline>150),"Review recent template or org object adds; recompute baselines if scope grew legitimately.",severity_bucket=="WARNING","Investigate one missed interval, poll skew, or slow copy; compare to change window.",1=1,"Continue baseline collection; no alert.")
| lookup ucs_backup_policy_owners.csv ucsm_domain policy_name OUTPUT business_service,oncall_pager,team_email,runbook_url
| table ucsm_domain,policy_name,policy_type,last_backup_age_hours,exp_h,file_size_mb,baseline_median_mb,pct_of_baseline,severity_bucket,recommended_action,miss_ratio,remote_key,business_service,oncall_pager,team_email,runbook_url
| sort 0 severity_bucket, - last_backup_age_hours
```

## Visualization

(1) Table of `ucsm_domain`, `policy_name`, `last_backup_age_hours`, `file_size_mb`, `pct_of_baseline`, `severity_bucket`, `recommended_action` with a colour range on `severity_bucket`. (2) Single value: count of `CRITICAL` in last 24h. (3) Timechart of `file_size_mb` for one high-value `policy_name` to watch slow growth after large template work. (4) A companion panel: `cisco:ucs:syslog` `backup` keyword frequency for human-readable failure reasons next to the numeric panel.

## Known False Positives

• The **first week** after you cut over from “syslog only” to HEC with `cisco:ucs:mgmtbackup` often shows **no `pct_of_baseline` row** because `baseline_median_mb` is still null: expect INFO-only rows; finish four weeks of stable exports before you bind paging to `pct_of_baseline` math.
• A TAC or support bundle export that the operator reuses the same `remoteFile` name for can make one interval look like a size spike: correlate with a ticket id in your change system and, if the export type toggled from logical-configuration to all-configuration on purpose, add a new lookup row, not a sev-1 on hardware.
• **UCSM cluster failover** in the same minute as a poll can yield one stale `lastBackup` read: wait a second poll, compare both fabric interconnect management paths, and do not reimage an FI off one lagging timestamp.
• **Daylight-saving clock boundaries** in the `lastBackup` string versus `now()` on the search head: if you see 55–70 minute `miss_ratio` in March or November, confirm `TZ=America/` settings in `props.conf` and the forwarder, not a “missed” policy.
• A policy **intentionally admin-disabled** during a wide change (maintenance) shows up as a huge `last_backup_age_hours` even though the business approved no exports: you must add `(ucsm_domain,policy_name)` to `ucs_suppressed_backup_policies.csv` (optional companion lookup) for that window, not silence the entire UC.
• NFS or SMB replication to a DR filer lags: Splunk on the “primary” export host can show a file tens of minutes smaller than the real DR copy. Move the `backup:filesystem` forwarder to the filer that is the contractual restore point, or add a second row keyed on `remote_key_dr` in the join, not a blind threshold cut.
• Gzip or other wrapper layers that append `.gz` and change size month to month: your baseline median is flat wrong unless you key `remote_key` to the final filename actually listed in `mgmtBackup.remoteFile` after compression.
• **Cisco Intersight** co-management where UCSM is still the trigger but Central schedules the work: a quiet UCSM row plus noisy Central is not a “green UCSM” story—verify Central's own job state before you close the book on DR readiness.
• A UCSM read-only API user with rights to `mgmtBackup` but not to a specific org: you get a consistent empty poll with HTTP 200 and JSON errors inside—Splunk will show *no* `mgmtBackup` event, a data quality incident, not a zero-size file.
• **Test domains** with weekly schedules that skip five weeks on purpose: mark them as non-prod in `ucs_backup_baseline` with a very large `expected_interval_hours` to avoid comparing lab cadence to production SLAs; this is different from a single-fabric A/B vNIC false positive in UC-19.1.36 and different from a port-channel error ramp in UC-19.1.13.
